free_text: 190 MeV proton-induced symmetric and asymmetric
fission
Energy signals and timing
signals relative to the cyclotron Rf and between
detectors were used for fragment identification.
. Fission fragments were detected in
a 163-cm diameter scattering chamber using solid-state
detectors.
Typically, a single 300-mm**2 Si detector was placed
on one detector arm, at 90 degrees, about 15 cm from
the target. An array of three 600-mm**2 Si detectors
was located about 30 cm away on an opposing detector
arm. All detectors were not more than 100 micrometers
thick.
The fixed angle of 300mm2 Si detector at 90deg in subs.
2,4,7,9,11,13 is  denoted as ANG1, relative angle of
oposing detector is denoted as ANG2.
Energy and relative mass spectra calibrated using
252Cf source.
Errors due to assumption that angular
distributions are isotropic.
Data reader uncertainties are much smaller.
. Relative fragment mass distributions at a
few angles.
No reply from F. Becchetti to author proof.
Converted from data set O0612 compiled at
CAJaD and updated.
SH Added digitized data from fig. 5 of the
reference.
Chloride or fluoride compounds of natural Ce, 400
microg/cm2, were evaporated onto thin carbon foils.
The CeF(3) material was obtained in ultra-pure form
with <10**(-7) parts heavy element contamination.
The target was placed at 45 degrees with respect to
the incident beam.
Data taken from Fig. 5 of the  reference.
REACTION, data heading, angle updated.
SH Digitized data added
Chloride or fluoride compounds of natural Ce, 400
microg/cm2 were evaporated onto thin carbon foils.
The CeF(3) material was obtained in ultra-pure form
with <10**(-7) parts heavy element contamination.
The target was placed at 45 degrees with respect to
the incident beam.
. Calculated from data at about 90 degrees,
assuming nearly isotropic angular distributions.
Graph of fission probability vs Z**2/A. Data
are digitized by compiler and fission cross sections
are derived using total cross sections calculated
using semi-empirical formula for geometric cross
section given by authors. (Khlopin Institute - AP).
Reaction corrected.
